frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment: Bro, whatever people say, you also have to be practical. If a girl is travelling alone late night or going to random places with drinks involved, obviously family will worry because zamaana is not that great and men won't change overnight. Better to be careful, share live location, avoid staying out too late and go with trusted people instead of taking unnecessary risks.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women's safety as their responsibility and normalizes male predatory behavior as inevitable rather than holding men accountable. It uses protective/practical language to justify restricting women's movement and behavior.
original_frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
persona_text: You are Karthik. Age 31. In a relationship. A man in Hyderabad. You are posting anonymously on Reddit in a thread where men are discussing what they find attractive in women. The thread has drifted into a conversation about makeup. You are sharing your opinion on women who wear a lot of makeup versus women who go natural and what each choice says about the woman's personality and confidence and honesty and what kind of woman you personally prefer and why natural is always better.
